channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: B.C. government drops confidence vote plan on proposed changes to Indigenous rights legislation
item.description: British Columbia Premier David Eby has withdrawn his plan to make proposed amendments to the province’s Indigenous rights legislation a confidence vote, a move that would have risked triggering a prov...

item.title: Former NATO chief warns of serious risks to UK national security and defence readiness
item.description: Lord George Robertson, former NATO Secretary General and author of the UK government's Strategic Defence Review (SDR), is expected to deliver a strongly worded speech warning that Britain's national s...

item.title: Study Reveals Sex-Specific Health Risks in Obesity: Men Show More Visceral Fat and Liver Stress, Women Have Higher Inflammation and Cholesterol
item.description: New research presented at the European Congress on Obesity highlights significant differences in how obesity affects men and women. According to scientists from Dokuz Eylul University in Turkey, men w...

item.title: 47 Canberra athletes to watch in AFL, soccer and netball for the 2026 winter season
item.description: Canberra's winter sports season has kicked off, with local Aussie rules, soccer and netball competitions underway for 2026. The Canberra Times has spoken to officials across the capital to compile a l...

item.title: Pakistan launches second nationwide polio vaccination drive targeting 45 million children
item.description: Pakistan has launched its second nationwide polio vaccination campaign of the year, aiming to immunise more than 45 million children under the age of five across the country. The campaign began on Mon...

item.title: Berekum Chelsea winger Dominic Frimpong killed in armed robbery attack on team bus in Ghana
item.description: Berekum Chelsea winger Dominic Frimpong has been killed following a tragic armed robbery attack on his team bus as they were returning from a Ghana Premier League match. The incident happened on Sunda...

item.title: US Congressman Resigns Amid Sexual Assault Allegations
item.description: A U.S. Democratic congressman has resigned from his position following allegations of sexual assault. The details surrounding the allegations remain somewhat limited, with the congressman citing a nee...

item.title: Suspect in Zamboanga robbery-murder arrested in Basilan, claims businessman ordered killing
item.description: A 21-year-old suspect in the brutal robbery and killing of a Lebanese businessman and his Filipina wife has been arrested in Lantawan, Basilan after a coordinated police operation based on informant t...

item.title: Call for international monitors as pre-election violence rises in Kenya
item.description: In this strong opinion piece, veteran journalist Macharia Gaitho warns that Kenya is already seeing coordinated pre-election violence ahead of the 2027 polls. Illegal militia groups linked to politici...

item.title: New Opera Australia director Amy Lane outlines vision for company and reflects on the future of opera
item.description: Amy Lane, recently appointed director of opera at Opera Australia, has been introduced as a key figure in the company’s renewed artistic leadership team at a pivotal moment in its recovery and restruc...

item.title: Jammu father seeks return of son deported to Pakistan; High Court orders government action
item.description: The article tells the emotional and legal struggle of Sajjad Ahmad, a resident of Rajouri in Jammu and Kashmir, whose 19-year-old son Aasim Sajjad was deported to Pakistan following heightened securit...

item.title: 58-year-old Dobsonville man accused of raping 12-year-old girl who went to charge her phone at his house
item.description: A 58-year-old man from Dobsonville in Soweto, who was well-regarded in the community as a helpful church elder, has been arrested and charged with the rape of a 12-year-old girl. The incident allegedl...

item.title: Delimitation Proposal and Its Potential Impact on Southern Indian States
item.description: The article examines the political debate surrounding the Centre’s proposed delimitation exercise for Lok Sabha constituencies in India. According to the understanding of opposition parties, the propo...

item.title: Binani calls for respect of court order, rejects ADC congresses in Adamawa amid legal dispute
item.description: Senator Aishatu Ahmed Binani, a governorship aspirant under the African Democratic Congress (ADC) in Adamawa State, has openly criticised the conduct of the party’s ward, local government, and state c...

item.title: SHA introduces phased POMSF tariff system to standardise public officers’ healthcare reimbursement in Kenya
item.description: The Social Health Authority (SHA) in Kenya has begun implementing a new tariff system under the Public Officers Medical Scheme Fund (POMSF) aimed at standardising healthcare costs and improving reimbu...

item.title: House Committee Begins Impeachment Hearing for Vice President Sara Duterte
item.description: The House Committee on Justice is commencing a critical phase in the impeachment proceedings against Vice President Sara Duterte. The hearing, scheduled for Tuesday, April 14, 2026, will feature the p...

item.title: Man charged after Molotov cocktail attack on OpenAI CEO Sam Altman’s residence
item.description: A 20-year-old man has been charged following a targeted attack involving a Molotov cocktail thrown at the San Francisco home of OpenAI CEO Sam Altman. According to authorities, Daniel Moreno-Gama alle...

item.title: Strengthening Graduate Quality and Employability in Private Universities
item.description: In this opinion piece, the author reflects on the ongoing debate about whether graduates from private universities in Kenya are as competitive in the job market as their counterparts from public insti...

item.title: Analysis of Nigel Farage’s political prospects and the obstacles ahead
item.description: The article in question could not be accessed directly due to a server restriction, so the following summary is based on the available headline and general contextual understanding of the subject matt...

item.title: Nigeria Plans to Start Supplying Natural Gas to Abuja via AKK Pipeline from July
item.description: Nigeria fit soon begin to deliver natural gas to Abuja through the long-delayed Ajaokuta-Kaduna-Kano (AKK) gas pipeline by July this year, according to the Nigerian Upstream Petroleum Regulatory Commi...
